title: FT._LIST
description: Returns a list of all existing indexes

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility

content:
Returns a list of all existing indexes. The prefix `_` in the command indicates, this is a temporary command. In the future, a [`SCAN`]() type of command will be added, for use when a database contains a large number of indices. ## Examples ```sql FT._LIST 1) "idx" 2) "movies" 3) "imdb" ``` ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis Cloud
Flexible & Annual | Redis Cloud
Free & Fixed | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Supported | ✅ Supported | ✅ Supported | | ## Return information **RESP2:** [Array]() of index names as [simple strings](). **RESP3:** [Set]() of index names as [simple strings]().
intro:
Returns a list of all existing indexes.


The prefix `_` in the command indicates, this is a temporary command.

In the future, a [`SCAN`]() type of command will be added, for use when a database
contains a large number of indices.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Returns a list of all existing indexes.


The prefix `_` in the command indicates, this is a temporary command.

In the future, a [`SCAN`]() type of command will be added, for use when a database
contains a large number of indices.
